title: Outcome frontmatter
_comment: Schema for outcome primitives. An outcome is the observed result of a previously logged decision: what actually happened, how it compared to expectation, and what was learned. Downstream consumers (decision retrospective, /patterns, future outcome-rebuild aggregator) depend on these fields. Malformed YAML breaks the decision-to-outcome join. Includes the cross-type frontmatter contract (provenance, confidence, freshness_days, last_verified, source_count, memory_class, entity_ids) shared across all 8 typed memory primitives. memory_class default for outcome is episodic. entity_ids cross-source linking (slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear).

decision_id.description: Pointer to the decision file this outcome resolves. Filename, wikilink target, or stable id.

observed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the outcome was observed.

observed_outcome.description: What actually happened, in plain prose.

vs_expected.description: How the observed outcome compared to the expectation logged at decision time.

lessons_learned.description: Distilled lessons, one per array entry.

memory_class.description: Episodic captures one-time events; procedural captures repeatable, time-stable knowledge. Default for outcome is episodic.

entity_ids.description: Cross-source identifiers. Keys: slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear. Values: source-system IDs.
